scenes.title: Welcome — Student Roster
scenes.editIntent: Quick welcome scene. Student roster fades in showing 5 students (Emma, Liam, Sophia, Ava, Noah) with grade and service minutes. Right panel shows total load and time slots. Single narration line. No operator action. System displays and auto-advances after 8-10 seconds.

blocks.text: Let's build your schedule from the students you already support.

scenes.title: Load Your Students
scenes.editIntent: Data import setup. Split-screen: left shows messy text input area (simulated), right shows live preview cards that populate as animation runs. Line-by-line transformation from unstructured text to parsed student records. Narration explains the frictionless import. Button click triggers animation, then auto-advances. Shows Molly that data ingestion is effortless.

blocks.text: Just paste your student list — even if it's messy. I'll structure everything for you.

scenes.title: Generate Groups
scenes.editIntent: Operator action #1. Student cards shown. Prominent Generate Groups button. Clicking triggers processing spinner, groups are revealed (Group A: 2 Grade-3 students, Group B: 1 Grade-4, 1:1 Required: 2 students). System auto-advances after groups appear.

blocks.text: Five students. Mixed grades and constraints.
Click Generate Groups — the system takes it from there.

scenes.title: Build Weekly Schedule
scenes.editIntent: Operator action #2. Groups displayed on left. Build Weekly Schedule button prominent at bottom. Clicking triggers processing and reveals a full 5-day schedule grid. Status indicators confirm all minutes satisfied and no conflicts.

blocks.text: Click Build Schedule — watch a full week appear.

scenes.title: Add Student — Quick Confirm
scenes.editIntent: Operator action #3. New student Mason shown as a display-only confirmation card (Grade 3, 120 min/wk, Can group: Yes). Alert banner reads New Student Enrollment. No form fields — this feels like approval, not data entry. Click Add to Schedule triggers processing and signals the shell.

blocks.text: Grade three. Can group. One-twenty a week.
Click Add to Schedule — and watch what doesn't break.

scenes.title: Automatic Rebalance — Hero Moment
scenes.editIntent: THE HERO MOMENT. Zero operator action. 3-beat choreography: Beat 1 — Detect (new constraint message, 1.5s). Beat 2 — Adjust (Mason slides into Group A, schedule blocks glow green, 3-4s). Beat 3 — Confirm (header settles to Rebalanced, all constraints maintained message, 1-2s). Fully automatic. This is the sell.

blocks.text: Mason absorbed. All constraints still met.
This is what it was built for.

scenes.title: Before vs After — The Relief
scenes.editIntent: Side-by-side relief contrast. Without Help: starting over, conflicts, guesswork. With Scheduler: adjusts on the fly, prevents conflicts, validates in seconds. Focus is emotional — relief, not metrics. Three points each, progressive fade-in. Narration: No starting over. No conflicts. No guesswork.

scenes.title: Close — The Kind of Relief Educators Need
scenes.editIntent: Final emotional anchor. Centered hero headline with gradient text: This is the kind of relief busy educators need. Glowing orb background. Subheading: Add a student. Press a button. Move on with your day. Restart Demo and Close CTAs. Manual advance — presenter holds here.

blocks.text: This is the kind of relief busy educators need.
